content: When testing EADDRINUSE with two HTTP servers on macOS, use explicit '127.0.0.1' instead of 'localhost' as the host. On macOS, dns.lookup('localhost') can return either 127.0.0.1 or ::1 across different calls, causing the two servers to bind to different address families and not conflict, making the EADDRINUSE test pass spuriously.

vitest test files that test external CLI tools (bd, legio) should mock the client module with vi.mock() to avoid PATH dependency in test environment. For beads client: mock createBeadsClient() to return fake client with list/ready returning [], show throwing, create returning a predictable ID. This keeps existing tests passing while enabling new tests.

Pre-existing async test fix: vitest .toThrow() does not handle async functions — use .rejects.toThrow() for async code. The pattern expect(async () => { await fn() }).toThrow(Err) silently passes instead of catching the rejection.

Testing nudgeAgent invocation from mail.ts: nudgeAgent records a 'custom' event with data.type='nudge' to EventStore even on failed delivery. Use getTimeline/getByAgent to check for this event as proof nudgeAgent was called. Delivery always fails in test env (no tmux), but EventStore recording happens regardless.
SQLite datetime('now') has 1-second precision — ordering tests for chat sessions must wait >=1100ms between inserts to get distinct timestamps. Same applies to updatedAt timestamp assertions.

biome noUnusedImports is an unsafe fix (requires --write --unsafe). Pre-existing unused imports in test files must be removed manually. When a test file imports readFile but doesn't use it, remove it from the import statement.

vitest.config.ts global testTimeout: git-heavy tests (createTempGitRepo, worktree operations, merge operations) each take 1-5s. Under full-suite concurrency, the default 5000ms testTimeout causes flaky failures. Setting testTimeout: 15_000 in vitest.config.ts test block is the correct fix — per-describe { timeout: 15_000 } only helps targeted blocks and leaves other untouched describes vulnerable.

CoordinatorDeps._tmux DI extension pattern: add new tmux functions as optional fields (waitForTuiReady?) so existing test mocks that omit the field don't break. Production default uses real implementation. Callers use 'if (tmux.waitForTuiReady)' guard with sleep fallback.

Set global testTimeout: 15_000 and hookTimeout: 15_000 in vitest.config.ts. Many tests create temp git repos (1-5s each); under full-suite concurrency the default 5000ms causes flaky failures. Per-describe timeout is insufficient — use global config.

noUnusedImports is set to error in biome.json — unused imports in test files will fail bun run lint. When importing an export for documentation purposes in a test file, always add at least one assertion that uses it, or omit the import entirely.

Mail store insert uses JavaScript new Date().toISOString() (not SQLite datetime('now')) for created_at. Two inserts in the same millisecond can have identical timestamps making ORDER BY created_at order non-deterministic. Use .toContain() or set-membership checks rather than index-based order assertions when testing chronological order of mail messages inserted rapidly.

E2E chat-flow test pattern: use same mock setup as routes.test.ts (vi.stubGlobal Bun, vi.mock bun:sqlite → better-sqlite3, vi.mock beads/client, vi.mock worktree/tmux). Seed orchestrator-tmux.json at legioDir for coordinator chat POST tests. Seed sessions.db with agent in 'working' state for agent chat POST tests.
E2E test files (src/e2e/*.test.ts) must use same vi.mock/vi.stubGlobal shims as routes.test.ts for bun:sqlite compat. Import sort order matters for biome — keep imports alphabetical by path.

Ideas test pattern in routes.test.ts: makeIdea(overrides?) builds fixture Idea. seedIdeasFile(dir, ideas) writes ideas.json. makePutRequest/makeDeleteRequest/dispatchPut/dispatchDelete helpers for non-POST methods. readFileSync used inline to verify disk writes after mutations. Uses same vi.mock beads client with create: async () => 'bead-test-001' for backlog test.

waitForTuiReady tests: use postReadyDelay: 0 in all tests that do not need to verify the delay itself, to keep tests fast. The postReadyDelay option (default 500ms) is applied after TUI marker detection before returning.

waitForTuiReady tests must pass postReadyDelay: 0 to keep tests fast; omitting it incurs the default 500ms delay per poll cycle. Use TUI marker content (e.g. box-drawing chars) in mock capture-pane output, not generic text like '> ready'.

bun:sqlite shims are dead code: the server already uses better-sqlite3 directly. The vitest bunShims() plugin and vi.mock('bun:sqlite') blocks in route/e2e tests were vestigial from the Bun→Node migration and can be safely removed.

vitest.workspace.ts defines unit vs integration split: unit excludes 7 slow files (coordinator.test.ts, resolver.test.ts, merge.test.ts, clean.test.ts, gateway.test.ts, mulch/client.test.ts, worktree/manager.test.ts). npm run test:unit for fast feedback, npm test for full suite.

name: source-to-test-ratio
description: 85 test files vs 86 source files demonstrates 1:1 source-to-test ratio. Tests colocated (src/foo.ts → src/foo.test.ts), use real implementations (temp repos, sqlite :memory:), avoid mocks.
